channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: OYOSUBEB chair rejoices over freedom of abducted pupils
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
OYOSUBEB Chairman rejoices over the safe return of abducted Oyo pupils and teachers after 56 days, thanking all involved in their rescue.
Read More: https://punchng.com/oyosubeb-chair-rejoices-over-freedom-of-abducted-pupils/

item.title: Our uncle is never coming home, slain Oyo teacher’s family reacts after victims release
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Family of Michael Oyedokun, an Oyo teacher killed during a school abduction, expresses pain after other victims’ release, saying their uncle will never ret
Read More: https://punchng.com/our-uncle-is-never-coming-home-slain-oyo-teachers-family-reacts-after-victims-release/

item.title: NAFDAC warns Nigerians over banned energy drinks seized in Ghana
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
NAFDAC alerts Nigerians to two banned alcoholic energy drinks, Bel Ice Vodka Energy Drink and Cody’s Vody Energy Mix, seized in Ghana due to health risks.
Read More: https://punchng.com/nafdac-warns-nigerians-over-banned-energy-drinks-seized-in-ghana/

item.title: APM commends Makinde, security agencies over release of Oyo kidnapped victims
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
The Allied People’s Movement (APM) commends Governor Seyi Makinde and security agencies for the release of Oyo kidnapped victims. Read more on their safe r
Read More: https://punchng.com/apm-commends-makinde-security-agencies-over-release-of-oyo-kidnapped-victims/

item.title: ‘I Apologize’: Vinicius Jr breaks silence after Brazil’s World Cup heartbreak
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: "Wearing the national team jersey is the greatest pride of my life, and leaving a World Cup in the round of sixteen is a feeling that's hard to explain," he said, adding that "I know how much I prepared, how much I focused, how much I wanted this for you, for my family."

item.title: Bakolori Farmers Decry Quelea Invasion Into Farmlands
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: …Call For Govts’ Intervention The Chairman of the Bakalori Water Users Association (BWUA), Malam Yakubu Yahaya, has decried the Quelea invasion into Farmlands, saying farmers had exhausted all available techniques to curtail the situation. Farmers at the Bakolori Irrigation Scheme have c

item.title: OYOSUBEB Rejoices As Abducted Teachers, Pupils Regain Freedom
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The Chairman of the Oyo State Universal Basic Education Board (OYOSUBEB), Dr Nureni Aderemi Adeniran, has welcomed the safe return of the abducted teachers and pupils, describing their release as a moment of great relief and thanksgiving for the entire education community in the state. In a statemen

item.title: Abiodun raises funding, judicial reform concerns as state police bill advances
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Ogun State Governor, Prince Dapo Abiodun, has called for far-reaching judicial, fiscal and institutional reforms to ensure the success of the proposed State Police system, saying the initiative must go beyond creating new security structures to addressing the legal and financial frameworks that will

item.title: School abduction came hours after I declared for presidency – Makinde
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Oyo State Governor, Seyi Makinde has said the abduction of pupils and teachers in Oriire Local Government Area happened less than 24 hours after he declared his intention to contest the 2027 presidential election. The post School abduction came hours after I declared for presidency – Makinde appeare

item.title: Relief as husband welcomes release of Oyo principal after eight-week ordeal
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Professor Wale Alamu, the husband of Mrs Rachael Alamu, the principal who was among the students and teachers kidnapped in Oyo State's Oriire Local Government Area, has described the victims' release as the end of eight weeks of anguish, while urging the government to learn from the incident to prev

item.title: European Parliament calls for terrorist listing of Rapid Support Forces in Sudan
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: First, the European Parliament calls on EU member states to officially designate the Rapid Support Forces in Sudan a terrorist organisation. Then, Kenya's Rastafarian community waits with bated breath as the country's high court gears up to deliver a ruling on the legalisation of marijuana. And, Mo

item.title: Oyo confirms release of abducted Oriire pupils, teachers
item.dc:creator: BusinessDay
item.description: The Oyo State Government has announced the release of the pupils and teachers abducted from Oriire Local Government Area, describing read more Oyo confirms release of abducted Oriire pupils, teachers

item.title: After years of delays, Tinubu delivers Wasa relocation promise to Apo mechanics, traders
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The long-awaited relocation of mechanics, artisans and traders from the Apo Mechanic Village to the Wasa District in Abuja has finally become a reality, ending years of unfulfilled promises by successive administrations. President Bola Ahmed Tinubu, on Friday, commissioned the access roads, power an

item.title: Donald Trump removes final members of independent US election commission
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: The dismissals leave the federal election body vacant as Trump presses for broader changes to US voting rules.

item.title: England favourites to reach semis, says Norway boss
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: England are favourites to reach the semi-finals of the World Cup, Norway boss Stale Solbakken has told BBC Sport.

item.title: S/Eagles defender Benjamin Fredrick signs new Brentford contract, promoted to first team
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: "He's already played senior international football and was a big part of Nigeria's team in their World Cup qualifying campaign." The post S/Eagles defender Benjamin Fredrick signs new Brentford contract, promoted to first team appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: EPL: Fredrick signs new deal at Brentford, promoted to first team
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Super Eagles defender Benjamin Fredrick has been promoted to Brentford’s first-team squad for the 2026/27 Premier League season. Fredrick has also signed a new four-year contract with the Bees, with a club option for a further year. The 21-year-old initially joined Brentford B on loan from Sim

item.title: Ndume to military: Shift focus to my district, rescue kidnapped students
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Senator Ali Ndume has appealed to the Nigerian military to intensify security operations in his senatorial district to secure the release of students abducted in Borno State. The post Ndume to military: Shift focus to my district, rescue kidnapped students appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: Men’s Wearhouse Owner Tailored Brands Files Publicly for US IPO
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Menswear company Tailored Brands Inc. filed publicly for an initial public offering, marking the latest step in its return to public markets.

item.title: Madugu Unveils Super Falcons Final Squad For WAFCON 2026
item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: Super Falcons of Nigeria head coach Justin Madugu has unveiled his final 25 players for the 2026 Women’s Africa Cup of Nations (WAFCON) in Morocco. The final squad, which is dominated by the WAFCON 2024 winning squad, was posted on the Super Falcons official X handle today (Friday). Among the player

Oba Aka

item.description: The Nigeria Centre for Disease Control and Prevention (NCDC) has confirmed 66 cases of Lassa fever and seven 66 cases of Lassa fever and seven deaths across the country over three consecutive epidemiological weeks. The latest situation reports released by the NCDC on Friday show that the infections

item.title: Oluwo hails Oyo kidnap victims’ rescue, warns against politicising insecurity
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
The Oluwo of Iwoland commends security agencies for the Oyo kidnap rescue, urging politicians to stop politicising insecurity and support national efforts.

item.title: Deadly blaze sweeps Spain, 12 dead, 23 missing
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: More than 150 firefighters, supported by 220 military personnel, helicopters and aircraft, have been deployed to battle the blaze, which has already consumed more than 3,200 hectares (about 7,900 acres) of land. Around 1,000 residents have been evacuated as emergency crews continue searching for tho

item.title: PDP, APM Welcome Release Of Oyo Kidnapped Children, Teachers
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The People’s Democratic Party (PDP) and the Allied People’s Movement (APM) have said the release of kidnapped Oyo schoolchildren and teachers was a welcome development. The children and their teachers were kidnapped nearly two months ago, and the opposition parties said their rescue, even thou

item.title: It’s big relief, Makinde hails release of abducted Oyo school victims, thanks Tinubu, security chiefs
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: From Juliana Taiwo-Obalonye, Abuja Governor Seyi Makinde of Oyo State has described the release of abducted persons in the state as a “big relief,” saying the state government and families had waited 57 days for their freedom. In a telephone interview with Channels Television aired on Politics Today

item.title: Meta’s stock roars back to life as it heads for its best week in years
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: Investors are bullish on Meta’s new low-cost AI pricing and infrastructure plans.

item.title: Kano Sharia Commission Threatens Sanctions Over Unapproved Friday Mosques
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Kano State Sharia Commission has warned that it will no longer tolerate the establishment of Jumu’ah (Friday) mosques without its official approval, saying anyone who violates the law will face appropriate sanctions. The Commission’s Second Commissioner, Sheikh Ali Danabba, issued the warn

item.title: Youth Sports Cost Debate Rekindled After US Soccer Exit
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Bloomberg's Randall Williams joins Scarlet Fu and Tom Keene on "Bloomberg Money." They discuss the growing cost of youth sports for American families, which has become a $40 billion-a-year private industry. The high cost is listed as a factor in why US Soccer can't take the next step on the world st

item.title: Official: Benjamin Fredrick signs long-term contract at Brentford
item.dc:creator: Soccernet NG
item.description: Super Eagles defender Benjamin Fredrick has committed his future to English Premier League Brentford by signing a long-term contract, Soccernet.ng reports. Fredrick was one of the best players from Nigeria's 2024 Under-20 World Cup squad. And his performances later earned him a transfer to Brentford

item.description: Head Coach Justine Madugu has named a 25-player Super Falcons squad led by captain Rasheedat Ajibade for the defence of Nigeria’s Women’s Africa Cup of Nations (WAFCON) title at the 14th edition of the tournament in Morocco. The squad, announced by the Nigeria Football Federation (NFF) o

item.title: A decade later, Pokémon Go finally made good on its original promise
item.dc:creator: The Verge
item.description: When Niantic dropped the first Pokémon Go trailer in 2015, it was hard to grasp how a bunch of players could work together to catch a pokémon like Mewtwo. But this week at the game's 10th anniversary event in New York City, Pokémon Go showed the world how it's done. Almost 2,000 playe

item.title: Striking UITH doctors call off action over assaulted female doctor

item.title: Oyo Police Rescue Abducted Couple Unhurt
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The Oyo State Police Command has rescued two persons who were abducted by kidnappers at Tiro Village, Irawo, in the Oke-Ogun area of Oyo State. According to the Police Public Relations Officer, Olayinka Ayanlade, “The victims, identified as Alhaji Busiru Booni and his wife, Alhaja Pitiku, were

item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: The Federal Government has announced that 39 more Nigerian nationals are being evacuated from South Africa as part of ongoing efforts to bring home citizens affected by the recent xenophobic attacks in the country. The update was provided on Friday by the Minister of State for Foreign Affairs, Bianc

item.title: Broos Confirms Departure As Bafana Bafana Coach
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Hugo Broos has officially ended his five-year tenure as head coach of South Africa’s national football team, though the 74-year-old Belgian has indicated he may remain involved with the South African Football Association in a scouting or advisory capacity. Broos, who took charge of Bafana Bafa

item.title: NPFL: Doma United will be ready for 2026-27 season – Ayeni

item.description: Doma United technical adviser, Ayodeji Ayeni, believes that with hard work, the team will be ready for the 2026-27 Nigeria Premier Football League (NPFL) season. Ayeni said his team is focused on achieving success in the new season. DAILY POST recalls that Ayeni was appointed to lead the Savannah Ti

item.title: 8 Kidnappers Arrested, Others Killed As Oyo Pupils, Teachers Regain Freedom — Presidency
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Presidency has disclosed that eight suspected kidnappers involved in the abduction of pupils and teachers in Oyo State have been arrested, while several others were killed during a security operation that led to the victims’ rescue. Special Adviser to the President on Information and Strat

item.description: Egypt’s squad has returned home to a hero’s welcome following their controversial exit from the World Cup.

item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Novak Djokovic says he wants to return to Wimbledon "at least one more time" and does not feel any pressure to continue his tennis career.

item.title: NYSC Osun passes out 1,702 corps members
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: The exercise was conducted simultaneously across the 30 local government areas and the Area Office in Osun State as part of the formal completion of the mandatory one-year national service.

item.title: Prince Harry visits King Charles III with Meghan and children for first time in years
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: King Charles III hosted Prince Harry and met with his family for the first time in years on Friday as they try to repair a rift that has persisted since a public, high-profile falling out that saw his youngest son and wife quit royal life and move to America six years ago.

item.description: The African Democratic Congress (ADC) governorship candidate in Jigawa State, Senator Sabo Nakudu, has rejected calls for his replacement over allegations of an ICPC indictment, describing the petition against him as a calculated attempt to create needless controversy. item.description: | By: Francis Onyemachi Nigeria has been ranked the leading country in Africa on the latest Global Index on Responsible AI, placing 38th in the world and first on the continent. Nigeria overtook Egypt and Kenya after climbing 42 places globally in just two years, rising from 80th globally in 2

item.title: Oyo school abduction followed my 2027 presidential declaration – Makinde
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: "For seven years, we didn't witness anything like this in Oyo State. I declared to run for the presidency of Nigeria at four o'clock, and by 9 a.m. the following morning, the children were abducted," Makinde said. item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: President Bola Tinubu has reacted to the successful rescue of the pupils and teachers abducted from Oriire community in Ogbomoso, Oyo, expressing delight over the operation carried out by security agencies. The President, in a statement on Friday, commended the military, the Department of State Serv

item.title: 39 more Nigerians return from South Africa on emergency evacuation flight
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Thirty-nine more Nigerians are on their way home from South Africa aboard an emergency evacuation flight arranged by Air Peace in collaboration with the Federal Government. The Ministry of Foreign Affairs disclosed this in a statement issued on Friday and signed by its spokesperson, Kimiebi Ebienfa.

item.title: What happened at France v Morocco
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: France faced Morocco in a rematch of the 2022 World Cup semi-final.

item.title: Vinicius apologizes to Brazilians for World Cup ‘frustration’
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: "I know how much I prepared, how much I focused, how much I wanted" the World Cup "for you, for my family," Real Madrid superstar Vinicius said in an Instagram message to fans. The post Vinicius apologizes to Brazilians for World Cup ‘frustration’ appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: Cubana Chief Priest opens up on Obi Cubana’s role in his success
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Nigerian nightlife entrepreneur Cubana Chief Priest has credited businessman Obi Cubana for playing a major role in his rise in the hospitality and entertainment industry. The post Cubana Chief Priest opens up on Obi Cubana’s role in his success appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: ‘Big relief’ – Makinde reacts to rescued Oyo schoolchildren, teachers, thanks Tinubu
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Governor Seyi Makinde of Oyo State has expressed relief after the rescue of schoolchildren and teachers kidnapped in Orire Local Government Area of the state. Speaking during a brief telephone interview on Politics Today on Channels Television on Friday, shortly after news of the rescue broke, Makin

item.title: EU says ‘addictive’ features on Instagram and Facebook breach its rules
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Bloc says some design features on platforms built by Meta can cause users' brains to go into 'autopilot mode'.

item.title: FG To Receive 39 More Nigerians From South Africa
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Nigerian federal government will receive another set of 39 Nigerians fleeing the xenophobic attacks in South Africa, following earlier evacuation efforts that led to the return of 1,141 Nigerians so far from the former Apartheid enclave. On Thursday, the fourth evacuation flight brought back 282

item.title: Presidency: Eight Kidnappers Arrested, Others Killed in Rescue Operation That Freed Oyo Students, Teachers
item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: . Says neither ransom was paid nor prisoners swapped . Tinubu happy at victims rescue, praises securiy agencies . Vows to get justice for victims including amily of Mr Oyedokun,

item.title: Power supply disruption in Abuja After invasion of AEDC’s Maitama station by land grabbers
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The Abuja Electricity Distribution Company, AEDC, said it regretted to inform customers in Maitama, Abuja, and surrounding areas that it is presently unable to supply electricity from its C2 Maitama Substation following the invasion and unlawful encroachment of the facility by land grabbers led by a

item.title: Notes from a NATO Summit: An Estonian Perspective
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: As the week draws to a close following the NATO summit in Ankara, a clearer picture is emerging of what was happening behind closed doors inside the Presidential Compound as the alliance's 32 leaders met. Among the revelations, reports from The Wall Street Journal and The Times of Israel suggest Pre

item.title: Jubilation On Social Media As Abducted Oyo Pupils, Teachers Regain Freedom
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: The release of the pupils and teachers abducted from schools in Oriire Local Government Area of Oyo State has sparked widespread celebrations on X, with many netizens expressing gratitude after the victims regained their freedom following over 50 days in captivity. Earlier this evening, the Special

item.title: Witnesses contest the Trump administration’s account of Texas ICE killing
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Those present say Lorenzo Salgado Araujo did not ram or 'weaponise' his vehicle, as the US government claimed.

item.title: Oyo abduction: Makinde thanks Tinubu, says no ransom paid
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: The Oyo State Government expressed gratitude over the safe return of the victims after weeks in captivity, saying their release underscored the state's commitment to tackling insecurity through principled and sustainable measures rather than financing criminal networks. The post Oyo abduction: Makin

item.title: Presidency: Abductors of Oyo pupils, teachers in DSS custody
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: Some of the persons involved in the abduction of 46 pupils and teachers in Oyo State are currently in custody of the Department of State Services (DSS), according to Presidential spokesman, Bayo Onanuga. Giving an update on the release of the victims, Onanuga revealed that eight kidnappers were arr

item.title: Best Gift Card Trading App in Nigeria 2026 – [GiftCardsToNaira]
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: GiftCardsToNaira has built a strong reputation by consistently delivering a smooth, secure, and customer-focused trading experience.

item.title: Adeyeye Bags African Leadership Award For Transforming Nigeria’s Drug Regulator
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: Prof. Mojisola Christianah Adeyeye, Director-General and Chief Executive Officer of the National Agency for Food and Drug Administration and Control (NAFDAC), has received the Special African Leadership Commendation Award at the 16th African Business Leadership Awards (ABLA) in London, in recognitio

item.title: Timeline Of Oriire School Abduction, Rescue
item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: The presidency said that the rescue operation was completed without any concession to the terrorists The post Timeline Of Oriire School Abduction, Rescue appeared first on Channels Television.

item.title: More Americans Are Getting Prenups, Even If They Aren't Rich
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Bloomberg's Nikki Waller joins Scarlet Fu and Tom Keene on "Bloomberg Money." Belle Burden’s blockbuster memoir, 'Strangers,' has riveted readers with the tale of her marriage’s sudden implosion and aftermath. They also discuss the rising popularity of prenups has been driven by changing financial d
